src.osir_client.osir_client.api package

Submodules

src.osir_client.osir_client.api.osir_api_case module

class src.osir_client.osir_client.api.osir_api_case.OsirApiCase(*, case_name: str | None = None, case_uuid: str | None = None)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

A class to represent and manage an OSIR API case.

This class provides methods to interact with OSIR API cases, including retrieving, listing, and creating cases. It also provides access to modules, profiles, handlers, and logs associated with the case.

_api

Private attribute for the OSIR API client.

Type:

OsirApiClient

case_name

The name of the case. Defaults to None.

Type:

Optional[str]

case_uuid

The UUID of the case. Defaults to None.

Type:

Optional[str]

_modules

Private attribute for the case’s modules. Defaults to None.

Type:

Optional[OsirApiModule]

_profiles

Private attribute for the case’s profiles. Defaults to None.

Type:

Optional[OsirApiProfile]

_handlers

Private attribute for the case’s handlers. Defaults to None.

Type:

Optional[OsirApiHandlers]

_log

Private attribute for the case’s logs. Defaults to None.

Type:

Optional[OsirApiTask]

Raises:

ValueError – If the case_name is not provided when creating a case.

case_name: str | None
case_uuid: str | None
create(case_name: str = None) → OsirApiCase[source]

Create a new case with the given name.

Parameters:

case_name (str, optional) – The name of the case to create. Defaults to None.

Returns:

The instance of the class with the new case created.

Return type:

OsirApiCase

Raises:

ValueError – If case_name is not provided and the instance’s case_name is None.

get(case_name: str, case_uuid: str | None = None) → OsirApiCase[source]

Retrieve and set up a case by its name or UUID.

Parameters:

  • case_name (str) – The name of the case to retrieve.

  • case_uuid (Optional[str]) – The UUID of the case to retrieve. Defaults to None.

Returns:

The instance of the class with the case set up if found.

Return type:

OsirApiCase

Note

Logs an error if the case is not found or if there is an exception.

property handlers: OsirApiHandlers

Get the handlers associated with the case.

Returns:

The handlers associated with the case.

Return type:

OsirApiHandlers

Note

Logs an error if the case is not set up (i.e., case_name and case_uuid are both None).

list() → None[source]

List all available cases.

Prints a formatted table of all cases, including their UUIDs and names.

Note

Logs a warning if no cases are found or if there is an exception.

property log: OsirApiTask

Get the logs associated with the case.

Returns:

The logs associated with the case.

Return type:

OsirApiTask

Note

Logs an error if the case is not set up (i.e., case_name and case_uuid are both None).

model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

model_post_init(context: Any, /) → None

This function is meant to behave like a BaseModel method to initialise private attributes.

It takes context as an argument since that’s what pydantic-core passes when calling it.

Parameters:

  • self – The BaseModel instance.

  • context – The context.

property modules: OsirApiModule

Get the modules associated with the case.

Returns:

The modules associated with the case.

Return type:

OsirApiModule

Note

Logs an error if the case is not set up (i.e., case_name and case_uuid are both None).

property profiles: OsirApiProfile

Get the profiles associated with the case.

Returns:

The profiles associated with the case.

Return type:

OsirApiProfile

Note

Logs an error if the case is not set up (i.e., case_name and case_uuid are both None).

model_post_init(context: Any, /) → None

This function is meant to behave like a BaseModel method to initialise private attributes.

It takes context as an argument since that’s what pydantic-core passes when calling it.

Parameters:

  • self – The BaseModel instance.

  • context – The context.

status(wait_end=False, timeout=300, interval=5)[source]

Fetches the handler’s status. If wait_end=True, waits for the status to become expected_status.

Parameters:

  • wait_end (bool) – If True, waits for the status to change.

  • timeout (int) – Maximum wait time in seconds.

  • interval (int) – Time between API requests in seconds.

Returns:

The API response.

Return type:

dict

model_post_init(context: Any, /) → None

This function is meant to behave like a BaseModel method to initialise private attributes.

It takes context as an argument since that’s what pydantic-core passes when calling it.

Parameters:

  • self – The BaseModel instance.

  • context – The context.

class src.osir_client.osir_client.api.osir_api_response.OsirApiResponse(response: Dict[str, Any])[source]

Bases: BaseModel

__init__(response: Dict[str, Any])[source]

Create a new model by parsing and validating input data from keyword arguments.

Raises [ValidationError][pydantic_core.ValidationError] if the input data cannot be validated to form a valid model.

self is explicitly positional-only to allow self as a field name.

info()[source]
